2604.21637.title: Multilinguality at the Edge: Developing Language Models for the Global South
2604.21637.paper_summary: Where and how language models (LMs) are deployed determines who can benefit from them. However, there are several challenges that prevent effective deployment of LMs in non-English-speaking and hardware constrained communities in the Global South. We call this challenge the last mile: the intersection of multilinguality and edge deployment, where the goals are aligned but the technical requirements often compete. Studying these two fields together is both a need, as linguistically diverse communities often face the most severe infrastructure constraints, and an opportunity, as edge and multilingual NLP research remain largely siloed. To understand the state of the art and the challenges of combining the two areas, we survey 232 papers that tackle this problem across the language modelling pipeline, from data collection to development and deployment. We also discuss open questions and provide actionable recommendations for different stakeholders in the NLP ecosystem. Finally, we hope that this work contributes to the development of inclusive and equitable language technologies.
2604.21637.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型（LM）在哪裡以及如何部署決定了誰能從中受益。<lb/>然而，存在幾個挑戰，阻礙了在全球南方的非英語社群和硬體受限社群中有效部署LM。我們將這一挑戰稱為最後一公里：多語言性和邊緣部署的交集，目標一致但技術需求往往相互競爭。<lb/>同時研究這兩個領域既是需求，因為語言多樣化的社群往往面臨最嚴重的基礎設施限制，也是機會，因為邊緣和多語言NLP研究仍然在很大程度上是孤立的。<lb/>為了了解這兩個領域的最新進展和結合的挑戰，我們調查了232篇論文，這些論文在語言建模流程中解決了這一問題，從數據收集到開發和部署。<lb/>我們還討論了未解決的問題，並為NLP生態系統中的不同利益相關者提供可行的建議。<lb/>最後，我們希望這項工作能促進包容和公平的語言技術的發展。
2604.21637.author: Lester James V. Miranda et.al.
2604.21637.authors: Lester James V. Miranda, Songbo Hu, Roi Reichart, Anna Korhonen
